name: Blue Ribbon Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 large Macintosh apples (peeled and thinly sliced)
1/4 cup chilled salted butter (cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
Crust: Combine flour and lemon zest in a medium bowl. Using a pastry cutter, cut butter into the flour until the dough resembles coarse meal. Gradually add ice water and mix until the dough can be gathered into a ball. Divide the dough in half, flatten into disks, wrap, and chill until firm.
Filling: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Mix together sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cornstarch in a large bowl. Add apples and toss until coated. On a floured surface, roll out one dough disk into a circle about 12 inches in diameter. Fold dough in half, then into quarters, and place the point of the folded crust in the center of a 9-inch pie plate, carefully unfolding it. Trim excess dough, leaving about 1 inch overhang. Pour in apple filling and sprinkle with butter pieces. Roll out second dough disk into a circle about 10 inches in diameter. Fold into quarters, place on top of the filling, folding any extra crust from the top layer over the bottom layer and crimp edges together. Make slits in the pie top to allow steam to escape; brush with egg wash. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon sugar. Bake for 20 minutes at 400°F (200°C), then reduce heat to 350°F (175°C) and bake another 30 minutes or until the crust is golden brown. Cool on a rack to room temperature.
